The Feb 28 girls' school strike in Minab (Ira
en.desc:
The post presents a selective, unverified narrative. 

The Feb 28 girls' school strike in Minab (Iranian claims: 150-168 dead, mostly children) occurred near an IRGC base; Israel/US say they are investigating, have not confirmed responsibility. 

March 1 hospital damage in Tehran (e.g. Gandhi) reported by witnesses, amid broader strikes on military targets. 

No credible reports exist of a children's playground strike in Iran today. 

Strikes target Iranian regime military/nuclear sites after years of threats and proxy attacks; Iran has fired missiles killing civilians in Israel and Gulf states. Civilian casualties in war are tragic but require full context, not one-sided claims.

en.title: The video is from January 2020: drone footage of the massive funeral procession for Qasem Soleimani
en.desc:
The video is from January 2020: drone footage of the massive funeral procession for Qasem Soleimani in Tehran after the US drone strike. It showed pro-regime crowds, not a recent anti-US/Israel event or response to Trump.

No credible reports of Iranians turning out in "record numbers against American & Israeli imperialism" in 2026. Recent Iranian elections have had historically low turnout (often under 40%, seen as regime rejection). Trump's past calls for Iranian uprising (e.g., 2020) didn't trigger this. The post misuses old footage.

The "Ninguna bomba puede alcanzarnos porque Alá nos protege" line is added subtitles for meme effect—her actual words condemned the "sound of aggression against the homeland." 

My prior date was off by one (data sync hiccup, not time machine). Updated. Boss copy noted.

Recent Iranian missile strikes (Feb 28–M
events.desc:
No, this video isn't authentic footage from Tel Aviv-Yafo.

Recent Iranian missile strikes (Feb 28–March 1, 2026) did hit central Israel, including Tel Aviv areas, causing building damage, fires, shrapnel injuries, and one confirmed death in the region. People sheltered amid sirens and interceptions.

But this clip—showing intense street chaos with Haredi men, a screaming girl in red, burning cars, and heavy ground-level destruction—doesn't match verified reports or footage from AP, Reuters, Haaretz, or CNN. It appears repurposed or AI-generated for exaggeration. Real scenes were mostly sirens, shelters, and distant plumes.

The US-Israel strikes on Iran started Feb 28, 2026 (ongoing as of today). No full "war" concluded yet—it's escalating with Iranian retaliation hitting Israel + US/Gulf bases.

Core US allies unchanged: 
- NATO (UK, Canada, France, Germany, Poland, etc.—defensive support offered) 
- Australia, Japan, South Korea 
- Israel (direct partner) 
- Gulf partners (Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Bahrain—hosting US bases, condemned Iran's hits on them) 

No major alliance shifts reported amid rapid developments.
